meaning: adv.毛骨悚然地,令人激动地
examples: I am first look at others to play, saw others play that thrillingly, is happy, oneself also want to attempt.
Instead he allows us to hover above his landscape and thrillingly feel both the particularities and the whole.
Perhaps even more thrillingly, when watching an orchestra perform we're granted extraordinary interpretative freedom.
At night, walking around the sharp turns of the alleys can feel thrillingly forbidding.
But it's not just newfound love that is making Jamie feel, thrillingly, like he's on a runaway train.
